title: Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Enhancement Act of 2025
short_title: Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Enhancement Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill requires the Department of Agriculture to hire a college to study a program that fights cattle fever ticks. These ticks spread a dangerous disease to cattle called bovine babesiosis or cattle fever. The study must look at how well the program stops the ticks from spreading and how it helps or hurts cattle farmers. The college must also check the treatments used and how much money the government spends on the program. After studying everything, the college must write a report with ideas to make the program work better.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Enhancement Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Review and Report
sections.text:
In this section, covered institution means a land grant college or university and a non land grant college of agriculture. Land grant college or university and non land grant college of agriculture have the same meanings as defined in section fourteen oh four of the National Agricultural Research Extension and Teaching Policy Act of nineteen seventy seven. Program means the Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program that is run by the Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service working together with the Texas Animal Health Commission. Secretary means the Secretary of Agriculture.

Within one year after this law is passed, the Secretary must offer to make an agreement with a covered institution to study the Program. The study must look at how well the Program works to stop and reduce the spread of cattle fever ticks. It must examine the good things the Program does for cattle producers and the difficulties cattle producers face in following Program rules. The study must review the treatment methods created and used by the Program. It must also look at the federal and state money given to support the Program in the most recent budget year, including money given to each research project connected to the Program.

Within one year after the Secretary and a covered institution make their agreement, the Secretary must give a report to the Committee on Agriculture Nutrition and Forestry of the Senate and the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives. The report must include the results of the study done under the agreement. The report must also include suggestions for making the Program better, including ways to make it easier for cattle producers to follow Program rules.
